title: Station economy is inherited, not manually chosen
rule: A colony station's economy outcome must be planned indirectly through body, facility, and market-link context rather than direct manual selection.
planner_implications: Never expose a fake choose-economy control.
unknowns: How tied or near-tied top economies should be surfaced

title: Colony-type ports inherit economy behavior
rule: Colony-type ports should be modeled as inheriting economy outcomes from local body context plus market links.
planner_implications: Colony-type ports need body and link analysis, not class-only assumptions.
unknowns: Exact post-build formula

title: Specialised ports retain an inherent economy baseline
rule: Specialised ports should not be treated as inheriting local body base economy in the same way as colony-type ports, even though links may still affect visible market behavior.
planner_implications: Compare specialised ports separately from colony-type ports.
unknowns: Which post-build effects remain class-specific

title: Local body type establishes base inheritable economies
rule: Body class establishes a first-pass inherited economy tendency for colony-type ports.
planner_implications: Body type must be part of economy projection.
unknowns: Direct live confirmation coverage per body class

title: Local body modifiers affect inherited economy tendency
rule: Rings, organics, and geological features can modify inherited economy tendencies.
planner_implications: Planetary features should not be ignored in economy projections.
unknowns: Full post-patch modifier behavior

title: Modifier stacking remains unverified
rule: The current repository preserves a non-stacking rule for local body modifiers, but this should remain provisional until directly verified.
planner_implications: Do not double-count modifiers without evidence.
unknowns: Whether any modifiers override, merge, or stack in edge cases

title: Strong links are preferred for targeted economy shaping
rule: Same-body strong links are the preferred mechanism for deliberately shaping a target market.
planner_implications: Strong-link architectures should be preferred for must-have outcomes.
testing_status: Strongly supported, still needs broader live verification
unknowns: Exact contribution values per facility combination

title: Weak links provide spillover, not guaranteed control
rule: Weak links are useful system-wide spillover but should not be treated as guaranteed market-control tools.
planner_implications: Weak-link-only repairs should be labeled experimental.
testing_status: Supported by routing model plus negative evidence
unknowns: Threshold where weak support becomes reliable

title: One weak Refinery link was insufficient in the Wregoe Dodec case
rule: In the tested Wregoe case, one weak Refinery link did not restore Steel, Titanium, or Aluminium at the Dodec.
planner_implications: Do not universalize single weak-link repair advice.
unknowns: Refinery threshold, rank order, and station-context effects

title: Preview equality does not prove post-build market equality
rule: Similar preview bars across station classes or layout variants do not prove that final services, missions, or markets are equal after construction.
planner_implications: Require post-build evidence before claiming class superiority.
testing_status: Confirmed as an uncertainty rule; final market comparison still unresolved
unknowns: Real post-build divergence

title: Specialised ports have fixed baseline economy strength
rule: Specialised ports use fixed baseline economic strength values rather than inheriting body-driven colony economies.
planner_implications: Compare specialised ports as fixed-economy anchors before layering strong and weak links on top.
testing_status: Extracted from Mega Guide v2.3.0; still worth spot-checking live on edge cases
unknowns: Whether every specialised subtype still matches the guide after recent patches

title: Specialised ports ignore local-body inheritable economies
rule: Specialised ports are not affected by the local body's base inheritable economy.
planner_implications: Do not pick a body for a specialised port expecting body inheritance to rescue an otherwise bad economy baseline.
unknowns: Whether any post-build UI still misleadingly implies body inheritance

title: Specialised ports ignore local-body inheritable modifiers
rule: Specialised ports are not affected by inheritable-economy modifiers such as rings, organics, or geologicals.
planner_implications: Keep ring, biological, and geological analysis out of specialised-port body projections unless live evidence says otherwise.
unknowns: Whether any UI summary conflates inheritable modifiers with link modifiers

title: Colony-type ports recompute from body plus inheritable modifiers
rule: Colony-type ports acquire economies from the combination of local-body base inheritable economy and applicable inheritable modifiers.
planner_implications: Treat colony-type port planning as body-first and link-second rather than station-class-first.
unknowns: Exact post-build weighting when several economies tie

title: Inheritable modifiers do not stack with each other
rule: The guide states that inheritable-economy modifiers do not stack with base body economies and do not stack with one another.
planner_implications: Do not double-count rings, organics, and geologicals in inherited-economy projections.

title: Contraband cannot be obtained through body inheritance
rule: No local body provides Contraband as an inheritable economy; contraband must come from strong/weak links or a specialised criminal outpost.
planner_implications: Reserve orbital planning space if contraband is a required outcome.
unknowns: Whether any future patch adds new direct contraband sources

title: Two dominant economies are the safe planning baseline
rule: The top two economies at a port protect their own supply from being netted out by the demand of the other economies present, which makes one- or two-dominant-economy designs the safest default planning baseline.
planner_implications: Default to one- or two-economy station designs for reliable commodity output.
testing_status: Supported by guide text and appendix overlap tables

title: Third and lower economies still create cannibalization pressure
rule: Demand from the top two economies and both supply and demand from lower-ranked economies continue to net against the rest of the market mix.
planner_implications: Treat any third-or-lower economy as an intentional tradeoff, not a harmless bonus.
testing_status: Supported by guide text and appendix overlap tables

title: Same-strength rank order is probably alphabetical but not proven
rule: The guide believes same-strength economy ordering is alphabetical, but marks that rule as still to be confirmed.
planner_implications: Tie cases need a warning flag instead of a hard recommendation.

title: Economy order can matter more than absolute boost
rule: Advanced system design guidance sometimes depends more on pushing an economy into rank position one or two than on maximizing its raw value.
planner_implications: Model rank-order objectives directly when doing advanced optimization instead of optimizing only for raw percentage totals.
testing_status: Extracted from Appendix A strategy guidance
